prompt:
Our GraphQL resolvers hit the users table once per field — a single feed page does 60 SELECTs for 60 author ids. I want a dataloader-style batcher (batcher.ts, export class BatchLoader<K, V>) but scoped to what we actually need, so please don't port the whole dataloader feature set. Notably: NO caching between windows — resolver freshness bugs burned us before, so a key requested in a later window hits batchFn again.

Shape: new BatchLoader(batchFn, { maxBatchSize?, schedule? }); loader.load(key) -> Promise<V>.

- All load() calls inside one scheduling window go out as ONE batchFn(keys) call, keys deduped and in first-request order; duplicate callers share the identical result.
- batchFn resolves to an array aligned with keys. An element that's an instanceof Error rejects just that key's promise. A wrong-length array is a batchFn bug: reject the whole batch with an Error that says how many results were expected. batchFn itself rejecting rejects every key with that reason, and nothing about the failure sticks — next window retries.
- maxBatchSize (positive integer, TypeError otherwise): the moment the pending set reaches it, ship the batch — don't wait for the window to close — and later loads open a fresh window. A window whose batch already shipped must not dispatch again when its flush eventually fires.
- schedule is an injectable (flush) => void deciding when a window closes, defaulting to queueMicrotask. The tests inject a manual one to pin the windowing down.

`node --test test_batcher.ts` is the acceptance gate; the test file is off limits. No npm.
